title: Segment Map: PM operating workflows
market_scope_summary: PM operating systems for teams that already have execution tools but still reconstruct planning, status, and decision context manually.
segmentation_logic: Segments are separated by how much workflow fragmentation, governance burden, and AI adoption change the urgency of a PM operating layer.

segments.name: AI-forward product teams
segments.definition: Teams already experimenting with AI assistance in recurring PM workflows.
segments.core_job: Keep recurring PM communication and coordination work moving without rebuilding context each cycle.
struggle_moments: Weekly updates require manual reconstruction across tools
AI outputs are fast but still need trusted operating context
primary_value_drivers: Time saved on recurring PM outputs
Higher trust in AI-assisted coordination work
segments.buying_context: Usually bought by a PM leader or operator trying to remove recurring operational drag before adding broader AI complexity.
adoption_constraints: Must fit around existing work trackers
Needs believable traceability before automation is trusted

posture_fit.rationale: This segment will switch for sharper PM workflow outcomes without requiring ProductOS to replace every existing tool.
The segment is not asking for a broad platform owner yet.

segments.name: Tool-fragmented enterprise teams
segments.definition: Enterprise teams with multiple systems of record and high coordination overhead.
segments.core_job: Preserve decision, planning, and delivery context across multiple systems without losing the explanation trail.
struggle_moments: Leadership asks for a defensible story across disconnected tools
PMs cannot trace why work changed across planning and delivery
segments.buying_context: Typically needs PM leadership or product ops sponsorship because adoption affects existing systems and reporting norms.
adoption_constraints: Proof of governance and traceability is required

posture_fit.rationale: This segment is attractive if ProductOS proves coexistence with incumbent trackers.
A governance-heavy wedge can win before platform breadth is necessary.

disqualifiers: Do not enter if the buyer expects ProductOS to replace every incumbent system immediately
